QUICK START GUIDE
STEP 1: ENTER PERSONAL INFORMATION
1. Go to the 'Input Data' sheet
2. Select your Filing Status from the dropdown (Single, MFJ, MFS, HOH)
3. Select your State of Residence from the dropdown
4. Enter your business type if self-employed
5. Enter number of dependents
STEP 2: ENTER PRIOR YEAR TAX INFORMATION
1. Find your prior year tax return (Form 1040)
2. Enter AGI from Line 11
3. Enter Total Tax from Line 24
4. Enter any estimated payments you made
5. Enter refund received (positive) or balance due (negative)
STEP 3: PROJECT CURRENT YEAR INCOME
1. Enter expected income for each quarter
2. Enter expected business expenses by quarter
3. Enter other income sources
4. Enter retirement contributions planned
Note: Be conservative - it's better to overestimate tax
STEP 4: REVIEW DASHBOARD
1. Check your total estimated tax due
2. Verify safe harbor amount is met
3. Note payment amounts and due dates
4. Review any alerts or warnings
STEP 5: MAKE PAYMENTS
1. Use Payment Tracker to record payments made
2. Print vouchers from Vouchers sheet if mailing payments
3. Or pay online at IRS.gov/DirectPay
4. Don't forget state estimated payments!
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
Q: What is safe harbor?
A: Safe harbor protects you from underpayment penalties. You meet it by paying:
- 100% of prior year tax (110% if prior AGI > $150,000), OR
- 90% of current year tax
Q: When are quarterly payments due?
A: 2024 Payment Due Dates:
- Q1: January 15 2024
- Q2: June 17 2024 (not April 15!)
- Q3: September 16 2024
- Q4: January 15 2025
Q: How do I avoid penalties?
A: Three key strategies:
1. Make payments by due dates
2. Meet safe harbor requirements
3. Pay at least 90% of actual tax due
Q: Should I pay federal and state together?
A: No, pay separately:
- Federal: Pay to United States Treasury
- State: Pay to your state tax authority
Q: What if my income changes during the year?
A: Update projections in Input Data sheet and:
- Increase remaining payments if income goes up
- Can reduce if income drops (but be careful!)
- Use Scenarios sheet to test different options
Q: Do I need to file quarterly returns?
A: No, you just make payments. File annual return.
UNDERSTANDING THE CALCULATIONS
Federal Tax Calculation:
1. Total Income - Business Expenses = Net Income
2. Net Income - Deductions = Taxable Income
3. Apply tax brackets to get Income Tax
4. Add Self-Employment Tax (15.3% of 92.35% of net SE income)
5. Subtract any credits
Self-Employment Tax:
- Social Security: 12.4% on first $168600 (2024)
- Medicare: 2.9% on all income
- Additional Medicare: 0.9% over $200k (single) or $250k (MFJ)
TIPS FOR SUCCESS
1. Update quarterly as you get actual numbers
2. Set aside tax money in separate account
3. Pay a little extra to create cushion
4. Keep records of all payments made
5. Review scenarios for tax planning opportunities
